  • Search

Search

Find the CSS decision you need

Search by problem, pattern, or architectural decision. Useful searches include specificity, utilities, state, modifiers, legacy CSS, @scope, Tailwind, tokens, and hacks.

Search Start here Common problems Quick routes

Search the docs

Search for the question you have, not only the page title. Try terms like specificity, state, utility, legacy, scope, or tokens.

Good starting points

What is LSCSS?

Start here if you need the short explanation before choosing a route.

Principles

Read the non-negotiables behind ownership, layers, tokens, utilities, and hacks.

Decision trees

Use these when you need to decide where CSS belongs right now.

Common problems

Why is this selector winning?

Check layers, ownership, and selector intent before increasing specificity.

Utility or component?

Use practical rules when repeated utility stacks start looking suspicious.

Modifier or state?

Separate permanent variants from temporary UI behaviour.

How do I improve legacy CSS?

Contain old styles and improve active work without rewriting everything.

What does bad CSS look like?

Recognise specificity ladders, random values, utility overload, and permanent hacks.

How do I audit a codebase?

Review ownership, tokens, components, state, legacy, hacks, and delivery risk.

Quick routes

Show me examples

Before and after code for selectors, utilities, state, legacy, and hacks.

Compare approaches

Compare LSCSS with BEM, Tailwind, CSS Modules, utility-first CSS, and @scope.

Introduce this to a team

Start adoption without disruptive rewrites or documentation overhead.

LSCSS

Layered Semantic CSS. A practical methodology for calm, maintainable front-end architecture.

Evolves with real delivery. Change what does not fit your codebase.

Learn Apply Teams Writing Modern CSS Search
